2606.31919.title: MVP-Nav: Multi-layer Value Map Planner Navigator
2606.31919.paper_summary: Zero-shot Object Goal Navigation (ZSON) with RGB-only perception poses a fundamental challenge for embodied agents, as the absence of explicit depth information introduces severe physical uncertainty and semantic-physical misalignment. Existing approaches either rely on high-level semantic reasoning without geometric grounding or learn end-to-end policies that lack explicit physical constraints, often resulting in semantically plausible but physically unsafe behaviors. In this paper, we propose MVP-Nav, a physical-aware RGB-only navigation framework that aligns perception, planning, and control with the real 3D world. MVP-Nav reconstructs explicit physical occupancy from monocular observations by leveraging 3D foundation models to project 2D semantic instances into 3D oriented bounding boxes, forming a global spatial semantic representation. To unify high-level semantic reasoning and low-level physical constraints, we introduce a Multi-layer Value Map (MVM) that integrates semantic priorities and reconstructed geometry into a shared cost space, enabling physically grounded geometric planning. Extensive experiments on zero-shot object navigation benchmarks demonstrate that MVP-Nav significantly outperforms existing depth-free methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance and validating that structured physical priors can effectively compensate for the absence of active depth sensors.
2606.31919.paper_summary_zh: 零-shot 物體目標導航（ZSON）僅依賴 RGB 感知對於具身代理來說構成了根本挑戰，因為缺乏明確的深度信息引入了嚴重的物理不確定性和語義-物理不對齊。現有的方法要麼依賴於沒有幾何基礎的高層語義推理，要麼學習缺乏明確物理約束的端到端策略，這通常導致語義上合理但物理上不安全的行為。在本文中，我們提出了 MVP-Nav，一種物理感知的僅 RGB 導航框架，將感知、規劃和控制與真實的 3D 世界對齊。MVP-Nav 通過利用 3D 基礎模型從單眼觀察中重建明確的物理佔用，將 2D 語義實例投影到 3D 定向邊界框中，形成全球空間語義表示。為了統一高層語義推理和低層物理約束，我們引入了一個多層值地圖（MVM），將語義優先級和重建幾何集成到共享成本空間中，使得物理基礎的幾何規劃成為可能。在零-shot 物體導航基準上的廣泛實驗表明，MVP-Nav 顯著優於現有的無深度方法，實現了最先進的性能，並驗證了結構化物理先驗可以有效彌補缺乏主動深度傳感器的不足。
2606.31919.author: Wenyuan Xie et.al.
2606.31919.authors: Wenyuan Xie, Shaokai Wu, Yijin Zhou, Yanbiao Ji, Guodong Zhang, Bayram Bayramli, Qiuchang Li, Xunchu Zhou, Yue Ding, Hongtao Lu
